problem.title: Convert Sorted List to Binary Search Tree
problem.description: Given the head of a singly linked list where elements are sorted in ascending order, convert it to a height balanced BST.
assumptions: Input is a sorted singly linked list.

example.explanation: Find mid of linked list as root, recursively build left and right halves.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Inorder Simulation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: First find the total length of the list, then simulate an inorder traversal: recursively build the left subtree (which advances the list pointer to the median node), create the root from the current list node, advance the pointer, then build the right subtree. This processes each node exactly once, matching the inorder sequence of the resulting BST.
solutions.code_python:
# class TreeNode: def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.left=self.right=None
# class ListNode: def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.next=None
from typing import Optional

def sortedListToBST(head: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    # Collect values then use sorted-array approach
    vals = []
    node = head
    while node:
        vals.append(node.val)
        node = node.next

    def build(left: int, right: int) -> Optional[object]:
        if left > right:
            return None
        mid = left + (right - left) // 2
        root = type('TreeNode', (), {'val': vals[mid], 'left': None, 'right': None})()
        root.left = build(left, mid - 1)
        root.right = build(mid + 1, right)
        return root

    return build(0, len(vals) - 1)

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Inorder Simulation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: First find the total length of the list, then simulate an inorder traversal: recursively build the left subtree (which advances the list pointer to the median node), create the root from the current list node, advance the pointer, then build the right subtree. This processes each node exactly once, matching the inorder sequence of the resulting BST.
solutions.code_python:
# class TreeNode: def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.left=self.right=None
# class ListNode: def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.next=None
from typing import Optional

def sortedListToBST(head: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    # Collect values then use sorted-array approach
    vals = []
    node = head
    while node:
        vals.append(node.val)
        node = node.next

    def build(left: int, right: int) -> Optional[object]:
        if left > right:
            return None
        mid = left + (right - left) // 2
        root = type('TreeNode', (), {'val': vals[mid], 'left': None, 'right': None})()
        root.left = build(left, mid - 1)
        root.right = build(mid + 1, right)
        return root

    return build(0, len(vals) - 1)
